mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as revenue expectations rise.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.5% signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and debt-service costs, compressing S&P 500 valuations and producing negative returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate generate an overheating signal that prompts Fed repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return is non-negative during months when both INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls.

mechanism_narrative: During low interest rate regimes, month-over-month growth in retail sales directly boosts expected corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because financing costs remain anchored.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with RSAFS growth while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month changes in industrial production capture real-time manufacturing surprises that predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days before broader macro data fully diffuse into prices.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and 5-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,200 thousand units signal durable construction demand and backward-linked commodity and employment strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return after HOUST exceeds 1200 is not significantly greater than when HOUST is at or below 1200 (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month acceleration in consumer price inflation exceeding 0.5 percentage points erodes real earnings expectations and triggers monetary policy repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the 3-month CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.7% that falls at least 0.2 percentage points in three months signals excessive labor market tightness, squeezing corporate margins via wage costs and generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 4.7% and falling rapidly.
